title: Not All Proofs Are Equal: Evaluating LLM Proof Quality Beyond Correctness
abstract: arXiv:2605.10379v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become capable mathematical problem-solvers, often producing correct proofs for challenging problems. However, correctness alone is not sufficient: mathematical proofs should also be clear, concise, insightful, and transferable to other problems. While this proof quality is subjective and depends on the reader and context, many of its components are concrete and broadly valued. In this work, we identify such components and introduce ProofRank, a benchmark curated from challenging mathematical competitions. ProofRank evaluates several scalable proxies of proof quality: (i) conciseness, measuring whether proofs avoid unnecessary steps; (ii) computational ease, measuring the extent to which a proof relies on tedious calculations; (iii) cognitive simplicity, measuring how accessible the used proof techniques are; (iv) diversity, measuring how varied a model's proofs for a single problem are; and (v) adap

Title: Not All Proofs Are Equal: Evaluating LLM Proof Quality Beyond Correctness
Abstract: arXiv:2605.10379v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become capable mathematical problem-solvers, often producing correct proofs for challenging problems. However, correctness alone is not sufficient: mathematical proofs should also be clear, concise, insightful, and transferable to other problems. While this proof quality is subjective and depends on the reader and context, many of its components are concrete and broadly valued. In this work, we identify such components and introduce ProofRank, a benchmark curated from challenging mathematical competitions. ProofRank evaluates several scalable proxies of proof quality: (i) conciseness, measuring whether proofs avoid unnecessary steps; (ii) computational ease, measuring the extent to which a proof relies on tedious calculations; (iii) cognitive simplicity, measuring how accessible the used proof techniques are; (iv) diversity, measuring how varied a model's proofs for a single problem are; and (v) adap
translated_title: 并非所有证明都同等重要：评估LLM证明质量超越正确性
translated_abstract: arXiv:2605.10379v2 公告类型：替换 摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）已成为能够解决数学问题的工具，常常能为具有挑战性的问题生成正确的证明。然而，仅凭正确性并不足够：数学证明还应清晰、简洁、富有洞察力，并能迁移到其他问题。尽管这种证明质量具有主观性，并取决于读者和语境，但其许多组成部分是具体且被广泛重视的。在这项工作中，我们确定了这些组成部分，并引入了ProofRank，这是一个从具有挑战性的数学竞赛中精选出的基准测试。ProofRank评估了证明质量的几个可扩展代理指标：（i）简洁性，衡量证明是否避免不必要的步骤；（ii）计算简便性，衡量证明依赖于繁琐计算的程度；（iii）认知简单性，衡量所用证明技术的可及性；（iv）多样性，衡量模型对同一问题生成的证明的多样化程度；以及（v）适应性。
tldr: 本文提出ProofRank基准，通过五个具体代理指标（简洁性、计算简便性、认知简单性、多样性和适应性）评估LLM生成数学证明的质量，超越了仅关注正确性的传统评价方式。
en_tdlr: This paper introduces ProofRank, a benchmark that evaluates the quality of LLM-generated mathematical proofs beyond mere correctness through five concrete proxy metrics: conciseness, computational ease, cognitive simplicity, diversity, and adaptability.
